key_insight: Orthogonalizing atom-centered enrichment functions within a finite element basis yields a systematically convergent DFT basis that is both more efficient and better conditioned for large-scale pseudopotential calculations.
engineering_relevance: This framework can cut the degrees of freedom, runtime, and memory needed for Kohn-Sham DFT, making large non-periodic electronic-structure simulations more practical on HPC systems.
method: Augment classical finite elements with compact atom-centered enrichment functions.
Orthogonalize enrichment functions against the finite element basis to improve conditioning and overlap-matrix inversion.
Solve the Kohn-Sham eigenproblem with a residual-based Chebyshev subspace iteration tolerant to approximate overlap inverses.
evidence_signal: Benchmarks up to 39,083 electrons show a 5–7× reduction in degrees of freedom, 5–9× lower computational cost, and 4–5× lower memory use versus the classical finite element basis.
watch_item: Performance depends on accurate and efficient construction of the orthogonalized overlap treatment, so implementation complexity and approximation quality in the inverse overlap step remain important concerns.
paper_title: Large-scale pseudopotential density functional theory calculations using orthogonalized enriched finite element basis
